title: Unit, Property, Replay, Headless SDL, Render, and Playtest Evidence · Behavior Case: Headless Sdl
summary: Tests assert implementation details, use real time and global random state, compare one screenshot, never inject failures, and call dummy video proof of usability. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the headless sdl increment in risk-layered automated and human game test suite. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-ca…
objectives: Use dummy video and audio drivers for adapter integration while labeling missing window, GPU, timing, device, and sensory evidence.
Generate bounded cases for wrap, collision symmetry, conservation, reconciliation, replay, and invalid input and retain smallest failures.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Asteroids team handles case 3975 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to layer pure unit and property tests, deterministic replay, headless SDL integration, render contracts, changed defects, and manual accessibility playtests; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. A defensible solution applies Headless Sdl, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to risk-layered automated and human game test suite.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-testing-headless-fixtures-5
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-testing-headless-fixtures-5.
# Case 9036: a controller-remapping accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save.
def worked_asteroids_testing_headless_fixtures_5(unit_cases: int = 24, property_cases: int = 40, headless_cases: int = 9, changed_defect_detected: bool = True, dummy_driver_verified: bool = True, render_contract_checked: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "playerworked5",
        "seed9036",
        "input5dekrow",
        "frameworked59036",
        "state9036worked5",
        "playtest5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if min(unit_cases, property_cases, headless_cases) < 1:
        return False, unit_cases, "test-layers"
    if not changed_defect_detected:
        return False, unit_cases, "mutation-survived"
    if not dummy_driver_verified:
        return False, headless_cases, "headless-gap"
    if not render_contract_checked:
        return False, headless_cases, "render-gap"
    total = unit_cases + property_cases + headless_cases
    assert total >= 3
    return True, total, "risk-layered-game-tests"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python Asteroids decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected frame, input, seam, collision, cue, save, package, or release case, then state which native pygame-ce, SDL, display, controller, audio, filesystem, GPU, accessibility playtest, installer, performance, recovery, or publication claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

options.text: Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional. That claim does not survive the repair the headless sdl defect evidence boundary.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Headless Sdl model · Theory Headless Sdl
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Headless Sdl: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 3261 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to layer pure unit and property tests, deterministic replay, headless SDL integration, render contracts, changed defects, and manual accessibility playtests; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Use dummy video and audio drivers for adapter integration while labeling missing window, GPU, timing, device, and sensory evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use dummy video and audio drivers for adapter integration while labeling missing window, GPU, timing, device, and sensory evidence.
